# veeam_backup_job (Resource)

Manages a Veeam Backup & Replication job.

Supported job types:
- **VSphereBackup** — VMware vSphere backup (requires `virtual_machines` block)
- **HyperVBackup** — Microsoft Hyper-V backup (requires `virtual_machines` block)
- **WindowsAgentBackup** — Veeam Agent for Windows (requires `agent_computers` block)
- **LinuxAgentBackup** — Veeam Agent for Linux (requires `agent_computers` block)



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `description` (String) Human-readable description. Required by the Veeam API.
- `name` (String) Unique job name as it appears in the Veeam console.
- `type` (String) Job type discriminator. Allowed values: `VSphereBackup`, `HyperVBackup`, `WindowsAgentBackup`, `LinuxAgentBackup`.

### Optional

- `agent_backup_mode` (String) Agent backup scope. Required for `WindowsAgentBackup` and `LinuxAgentBackup` job types. Allowed values: `EntireComputer`, `Volumes`, `FileLevel`.
- `agent_computers` (Attributes List) List of agent-managed computers or protection groups to include in the backup. Required for `WindowsAgentBackup` and `LinuxAgentBackup` job types. Obtain object IDs from the `veeam_protected_computers` data source or the Veeam console. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--agent_computers))
- `guest_processing` (Attributes) Application-aware processing and guest OS file indexing. Applies to `VSphereBackup` and `HyperVBackup` job types only. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--guest_processing))
- `is_high_priority` (Boolean) If `true`, the resource scheduler prioritises this job over other similar jobs and allocates resources to it first.
- `schedule` (Attributes) Job scheduling configuration. When omitted, the job must be started manually. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--schedule))
- `storage` (Attributes) Backup storage configuration. When omitted, Veeam applies server defaults. **Strongly recommended** to set explicitly. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--storage))
- `virtual_machines` (Attributes) Defines which VMs or containers the job protects. Required for `VSphereBackup` and `HyperVBackup` job types. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--virtual_machines))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Job identifier assigned by the server (UUID).
- `is_disabled` (Boolean) Whether the job is disabled (read-only; use the Veeam console to disable/enable jobs, or use the enable/disable API endpoints).

<a id="nestedatt--agent_computers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `agent_computers`

Required:

- `id` (String) UUID of the agent-managed object.
- `name` (String) Display name of the computer or protection group.
- `protection_group_id` (String) UUID of the protection group that contains this object. Obtain from the `veeam_protection_group` resource or data source.
- `type` (String) Object class. Common values: `ProtectionGroup`, `WindowsComputer`, `LinuxComputer`, `WindowsCluster`, `Domain`, `OrganizationUnit`.


<a id="nestedatt--guest_processing"></a>
### Nested Schema for `guest_processing`

Optional:

- `app_aware_enabled` (Boolean) If `true`, application-aware processing is enabled. Requires VMware Tools / Hyper-V Integration Services to be installed in each guest VM.
- `fs_indexing_enabled` (Boolean) If `true`, guest OS file indexing is enabled to allow file-level search inside backup archives.
- `interaction_proxy_auto_select` (Boolean) If `true` (default), Veeam automatically selects the guest interaction proxy.


<a id="nestedatt--schedule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule`

Optional:

- `after_job_enabled` (Boolean) If `true`, this job starts automatically after another job completes.
- `after_job_name` (String) Display **name** of the preceding job. The Veeam API v1.3 identifies chained jobs by name, not UUID.
- `daily_enabled` (Boolean) Enable daily schedule.
- `daily_kind` (String) Which days to run. Allowed values: `Everyday`, `Weekdays`, `SelectedDays`.
- `daily_local_time` (String) Daily start time in `HH:MM` format (server local time).
- `monthly_day_of_month` (Number) Day of the month (1–28) on which the job runs.
- `monthly_enabled` (Boolean) Enable monthly schedule.
- `monthly_local_time` (String) Monthly start time in `HH:MM` format.
- `periodically_enabled` (Boolean) Enable periodic (interval-based) schedule.
- `periodically_frequency` (Number) Number of time units between runs.
- `periodically_kind` (String) Time unit for the interval. Allowed values: `Hours`, `Minutes`, `Seconds`, `Days`.
- `retry_await_minutes` (Number) Wait time between retries in minutes (must be > 0).
- `retry_count` (Number) Number of retry attempts (must be > 0).
- `retry_enabled` (Boolean) If `true`, the job is retried automatically when it fails.
- `run_automatically` (Boolean) If `true`, the job runs on the configured schedule automatically.


<a id="nestedatt--storage"></a>
### Nested Schema for `storage`

Optional:

- `proxy_auto_select` (Boolean) If `true` (default), Veeam automatically selects the most suitable backup proxy.
- `repository_id` (String) UUID of the target backup repository. Obtain from the `veeam_backup_repositories` data source.
- `retention_quantity` (Number) Number of restore points or days to retain (must be greater than zero).
- `retention_type` (String) Retention policy type. Allowed values: `RestorePoints`, `Days`.


<a id="nestedatt--virtual_machines"></a>
### Nested Schema for `virtual_machines`

Required:

- `includes` (Attributes List) List of VMware vSphere or Hyper-V objects to include in the backup. At least one entry is required. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--virtual_machines--includes))

Optional:

- `exclude_templates` (Boolean) If `true`, all VM templates are excluded from the backup scope.

<a id="nestedatt--virtual_machines--includes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `virtual_machines.includes`

Required:

- `name` (String) Display name of the inventory object (VM name, folder name, datacenter name, etc.).
- `platform` (String) Hypervisor platform. Allowed values: `VSphere`, `HyperV`.

Optional:

- `host_name` (String) FQDN or IP address of the vCenter Server or ESXi host that owns this object.
- `object_id` (String) vSphere MoRef ID (e.g. `vm-101`, `domain-c12`). Required for all objects except vCenter Servers and standalone ESXi hosts.
- `type` (String) Object type within the platform. vSphere examples: `VirtualMachine`, `Folder`, `Datacenter`, `Cluster`, `Host`, `ResourcePool`, `VirtualApp`, `Tag`. Leave empty to let the API infer the type.
